name: Capo → Delegate System
payload: role name, RelativeDelegateLinkLike, readOnly option; returns connected delegate instance

name: Delegate System → Data Bridge
payload: raw UPLC UplcData from on-chain datum; returns typed TypeScript object

name: StellarContract → Script Bundle
payload: bundle class with configured params; returns compiled UplcProgram or precompiled scriptHash

name: StellarTxnContext → Network Clients
payload: finalized Tx objects, WalletSigningStrategy; returns submission confirmation

steps.action: connectDelegateWithOnchainRDLink readOnly - init only, no compilation
newReadDatum - pre-generated cast functions convert UPLC to TypeScript

name: Transaction Chaining (forward)
description: Primary transaction generates follow-on transactions via addlTxns. Later transactions use mkTcx callback with TxChainBuilder facade exposing virtual UTxOs from prior transactions.

name: Conditional Facade (evaluative)
description: Transaction facade created when need for transactions is uncertain. Situation evaluated, transactions conditionally added. mkTcx callbacks with TxChainBuilder facade.

name: Delegate Authority Inclusion
description: Internal tx workflow: locate delegate UUT at script address, spend it as input, return it as output. StellarTxnContext type S refined to prove authority presence. Chain of custody at transaction level.

name: Preparatory Facade (inverse chaining)
description: Target transaction needs prep work. Facade created, preparatory transactions added first, then target transaction. Target sees virtual UTxOs from preps via TxChainBuilder.

name: DelegatedDataContract Lifecycle Hooks
description: Extension points on DelegatedDataContract firing during transaction building (before-hooks) and after submission (after-hooks). Before-hooks are synchronous data transforms receiving frozen deep-cloned records and mutable tcx; after-hooks are async side-effect points. Existing: beforeCreate, beforeUpdate (both enriched with tcx and record protection). Planned: afterCreate, afterUpdate. Backlog: typed activity identity in hook contexts.

name: beforeCreate hook
description: Synchronous transform hook called in txnCreatingRecord() after merging defaults+id+type+caller data, before datum construction. Receives (record: TLike, context: createContext<TLike>) where record is a Helios-aware deep clone frozen to enforce the return-value contract, and context includes { activity, tcx }. tcx is mutable — hooks may call tcx.validFor() etc. Base class is passthrough. Subclasses override to normalize data for on-chain policy compliance.

name: beforeUpdate hook
description: Synchronous transform hook called in txnUpdatingRecord() after merging existing record with updated fields, before datum construction. Receives (record: TLike, context: updateContext<T>) where both record and context.original are Helios-aware deep clones frozen to enforce the return-value contract, and context includes { original, activity, tcx }. tcx is mutable. Base class is passthrough.

name: afterCreate / afterUpdate hooks (planned)
description: Planned async side-effect hooks firing after successful transaction submission. Unlike before-hooks (synchronous transforms), after-hooks would not affect the transaction result. Use cases: cache invalidation, notifications, logging. Requirements in DelegatedDataContract.reqts.md REQT-2.0.

name: Typed activity identity in hook contexts (backlog)
description: Backlog enhancement: createContext and updateContext should include type-safe activity variant name (keyof MintingActivity / keyof SpendingActivity from generated type bridge) and pre-serialization args. Requires union key extraction utility types and threading concrete activity types through DelegatedDataContract generics. Common case: no args (seeded minting). When activity construction is deferred via LazyRedeemerData (ARCH-b4gv3t4jyj), hooks get whatever partial args are known at invocation time.

name: Helios-aware record utilities
description: deepCloneRecord and deepFreezeRecord in src/util/deepCloneRecord.ts. Duck-typing heuristic: .copy() → call it (mutable Helios types like Address, Value, Assets), .toCbor() without .copy() → share by reference (immutable types like PubKeyHash, UplcData variants), plain objects/arrays → recurse, primitives → pass through. Freeze only freezes plain objects and arrays, not Helios instances. Tested with real Helios types.
